commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=010aef7cfb449bb46842a854778397bae24c9191)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new `hyperv` local-VM SSH lease provider with config, flags, registration, provider docs, and mocked provider tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: source inspection shows YAML/env target values are marked explicit in config loading, while Hyper-V only checks whether the CLI `--target` flag was set before converting Linux to Windows. Not applicable for the feature request itself because this is a new provider PR, not a current-main bug report.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can fix the explicit-target bug and docs mismatch; contributor-owned live Hyper-V proof still remains after that repair.

target.title: feat: add Hyper-V provider for local Windows VMs
target.body:
## Summary

Add a `hyperv` provider for running Crabbox workloads on local Windows VMs via Microsoft Hyper-V and PowerShell Direct — a Windows-native counterpart to the `tart` (macOS) provider, with no external CLI or cloud account needed.

- Generation 2 VMs cloned per-lease from a pre-configured VHDX template (ISO rejected — the template must already have OpenSSH + a known user)
- SSH public-key injection over **PowerShell Direct** (`Invoke-Command -VMName`) with retry + backoff; guest password passed via env var, never on the command line
- Full lease lifecycle: acquire, resolve, list, release, idle cleanup, doctor
- Native-Windows readiness probe (git + tar + work root), shared with the other Windows paths

## What to review

The implementation is ~1,300 lines across 4 files; the rest is tests and docs.

| Area | Files | Lines | What it does |
|------|-------|------:|-------------|
| **Provider** (review this) | `internal/providers/hyperv/{backend,core,flags,provider}.go` | ~1,224 | VM lifecycle: clone/resize/create/start, IP discovery, SSH-key injection, resolve, release, cleanup, doctor |
| **Wiring** | `internal/cli/config.go`, `internal/providers/all/all.go` | ~67 | `HyperVConfig` (YAML/env/CLI), provider registration |
| **Tests** | `internal/providers/hyperv/provider_test.go` | ~840 | 46 unit tests (mocked PowerShell runner) |
| **Docs** | `docs/providers/hyperv.md`, README + source-map | ~129 | Provider reference page and index entries |

## How it works

- **Acquire**: validate the Hyper-V switch → `Copy-Item` the VHDX template → grow-only `Resize-VHD` (never shrink) → `New-VM -Generation 2 -Path <vmDir>` → `Set-VM` CPU + **disable automatic checkpoints** → `Connect-VMNetworkAdapter` → `Start-VM` → inject SSH key via PowerShell Direct → wait for IP + SSH readiness
- **Resolve**: look up by lease-id / slug / instance name; live IP query with claim fallback
- **List**: `Get-VM` filtered to the `crabbox-` name prefix
- **Release**: `Stop-VM` + `Remove-VM`, then delete the boot VHDX and the per-VM config directory; real delete failures propagate, disk-cleanup failures warn
- **Cleanup**: garbage-collect stale `crabbox-` VMs past their idle timeout
- **Doctor**: verify Hyper-V is enabled and inventory is queryable; reports the configured image

Storage follows the user profile: VHDs in `%USERPROFILE%\Hyper-V\Virtual Hard Disks`, VM config/runtime in `%USERPROFILE%\Hyper-V\Virtual Machines` — both on the same drive, so neither lands on the system drive by default.

## Live host smoke (Windows 11 Pro, Hyper-V enabled, Go 1.26.4)

Built from branch head and run end-to-end against real Hyper-V. The host's `C:` was full (3.4 GB free), so `%USERPROFILE%` was redirected to `D:` to give the 42 GB template copy room — which also exercised the off-system-drive placement.

**doctor**
```
ok  provider  provider=hyperv hyperv=Enabled control_plane=local inventory=ready
              api=powershell mutation=false leases=0 image=...\WinDev2407Eval.vhdx ssh_probe=unchecked
```

**warmup → start** — `Start-VM` succeeded; the VM ran with its config and `.VMRS` memory file on `D:` (not `C:\ProgramData`), `AutomaticCheckpointsEnabled=False`, and no `.avhdx` differencing disk created:
```
VM crabbox-hyperv-proof-... : Running, 4096 MB
ConfigLocation : D:\...\Hyper-V\Virtual Machines\crabbox-hyperv-proof-...
```

**stop → release** — clean teardown, zero residue:
```
released instance=crabbox-hyperv-proof-...
crabbox VMs remaining: 0 ; VHD dir: empty ; VM config dir: empty
```

> SSH-readiness is not shown above: the WinDev evaluation image used for the smoke rejects PowerShell-Direct credentials until its first-boot OOBE completes (`The credential is invalid`). That is an image-provisioning property of the eval VHDX, not a provider issue — the provider connected, retried, and failed cleanly with a clear message. A template with OpenSSH + a ready local user completes the lease.

## Fixes addressed

Driven by ClawSweeper review rounds and the live smoke above:

1. **Off-system-drive placement** — `New-VM -Path` puts VM config + the multi-GB `.VMRS` on the same drive as the VHD, instead of defaulting to `C:\ProgramData` (which failed `Start-VM` on a full system drive).
2. **Grow-only resize** — `Resize-VHD` only grows a copied template, never shrinks it (shrinking a template with a larger virtual size corrupts the disk); fails fast with cleanup if the copy is unreadable.
3. **No checkpoint disk leak** — disable automatic checkpoints on lease VMs (client Hyper-V enables them by default), and `removeVM` always deletes the base VHDX, sweeps any `<name>_<guid>.avhdx`, and removes the per-VM config directory. Without this, every release leaked a disk-sized file.
4. **Honor explicit `--ssh-user`** — `applyDefaults` inherits an explicit SSH user before defaulting the guest user.
5. **TargetOS / explicit-target handling** — force `TargetOS=windows`; reject an explicit `--target` that conflicts with the provider's native OS; preserve explicit target config; never delete claims on a non-Windows host.
6. **Credential & robustness hygiene** — guest password via env var (not argv); fail fast on injection failure instead of burning the timeout; post-boot injection retry; populate `PublicNet.IPv4.IP`; shared Windows readiness check; doctor reports the image.
7. **Canonical name only** — removed unapproved provider aliases, keeping `hyperv`.

## Configuration

CLI flags: `--hyperv-image`, `--hyperv-cpu`, `--hyperv-memory`, `--hyperv-disk`, `--hyperv-switch`, `--hyperv-user`, `--hyperv-work-root`

YAML (`.crabbox.yaml`):
```yaml
hyperv:
  image: C:\Images\win-server.vhdx   # VHDX template with OpenSSH + a known user
  switch: Default Switch
  user: crabbox
  cpus: 4
  memory: 8192   # MB
  disk: 50       # GB; only grows the template, never shrinks
```

Env vars: `CRABBOX_HYPERV_IMAGE`, `CRABBOX_HYPERV_USER`, `CRABBOX_HYPERV_WORK_ROOT`, `CRABBOX_HYPERV_GUEST_PASSWORD` (for PowerShell-Direct injection).

## Not included (follow-up)

- **Configurable storage root** — storage currently follows `%USERPROFILE%`; a dedicated config knob (rather than profile redirection) would help hosts whose profile is on a full drive.
- **Differencing-disk leases** — each lease copies the full template; a differencing/parent-disk mode would avoid the per-lease copy.
- **ISO install / autounattend** — templates must be pre-provisioned VHDX; ISO is rejected.
- WSL2 mode is explicitly rejected (native Windows only).
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.body:
## Summary

Add a `hyperv` provider for running Crabbox workloads on local Windows VMs via Microsoft Hyper-V and PowerShell Direct — a Windows-native counterpart to the `tart` (macOS) provider, with no external CLI or cloud account needed.

- Generation 2 VMs cloned per-lease from a pre-configured VHDX template (ISO rejected — the template must already have OpenSSH + a known user)
- SSH public-key injection over **PowerShell Direct** (`Invoke-Command -VMName`) with retry + backoff; guest password passed via env var, never on the command line
- Full lease lifecycle: acquire, resolve, list, release, idle cleanup, doctor
- Native-Windows readiness probe (git + tar + work root), shared with the other Windows paths

## What to review

The implementation is ~1,300 lines across 4 files; the rest is tests and docs.

| Area | Files | Lines | What it does |
|------|-------|------:|-------------|
| **Provider** (review this) | `internal/providers/hyperv/{backend,core,flags,provider}.go` | ~1,224 | VM lifecycle: clone/resize/create/start, IP discovery, SSH-key injection, resolve, release, cleanup, doctor |
| **Wiring** | `internal/cli/config.go`, `internal/providers/all/all.go` | ~67 | `HyperVConfig` (YAML/env/CLI), provider registration |
| **Tests** | `internal/providers/hyperv/provider_test.go` | ~840 | 46 unit tests (mocked PowerShell runner) |
| **Docs** | `docs/providers/hyperv.md`, README + source-map | ~129 | Provider reference page and index entries |

## How it works

- **Acquire**: validate the Hyper-V switch → `Copy-Item` the VHDX template → grow-only `Resize-VHD` (never shrink) → `New-VM -Generation 2 -Path <vmDir>` → `Set-VM` CPU + **disable automatic checkpoints** → `Connect-VMNetworkAdapter` → `Start-VM` → inject SSH key via PowerShell Direct → wait for IP + SSH readiness
- **Resolve**: look up by lease-id / slug / instance name; live IP query with claim fallback
- **List**: `Get-VM` filtered to the `crabbox-` name prefix
- **Release**: `Stop-VM` + `Remove-VM`, then delete the boot VHDX and the per-VM config directory; real delete failures propagate, disk-cleanup failures warn
- **Cleanup**: garbage-collect stale `crabbox-` VMs past their idle timeout
- **Doctor**: verify Hyper-V is enabled and inventory is queryable; reports the configured image

Storage follows the user profile: VHDs in `%USERPROFILE%\Hyper-V\Virtual Hard Disks`, VM config/runtime in `%USERPROFILE%\Hyper-V\Virtual Machines` — both on the same drive, so neither lands on the system drive by default.

## Live host smoke (Windows 11 Pro, Hyper-V enabled, Go 1.26.4)

Built from branch head and run end-to-end against real Hyper-V. The host's `C:` was full (3.4 GB free), so `%USERPROFILE%` was redirected to `D:` to give the 42 GB template copy room — which also exercised the off-system-drive placement.

**doctor**
```
ok  provider  provider=hyperv hyperv=Enabled control_plane=local inventory=ready
              api=powershell mutation=false leases=0 image=...\WinDev2407Eval.vhdx ssh_probe=unchecked
```

**warmup → start** — `Start-VM` succeeded; the VM ran with its config and `.VMRS` memory file on `D:` (not `C:\ProgramData`), `AutomaticCheckpointsEnabled=False`, and no `.avhdx` differencing disk created:
```
VM crabbox-hyperv-proof-... : Running, 4096 MB
ConfigLocation : D:\...\Hyper-V\Virtual Machines\crabbox-hyperv-proof-...
```

**stop → release** — clean teardown, zero residue:
```
released instance=crabbox-hyperv-proof-...
crabbox VMs remaining: 0 ; VHD dir: empty ; VM config dir: empty
```

> SSH-readiness is not shown above: the WinDev evaluation image used for the smoke rejects PowerShell-Direct credentials until its first-boot OOBE completes (`The credential is invalid`). That is an image-provisioning property of the eval VHDX, not a provider issue — the provider connected, retried, and failed cleanly with a clear message. A template with OpenSSH + a ready local user completes the lease.

## Fixes addressed

Driven by ClawSweeper review rounds and the live smoke above:

1. **Off-system-drive placement** — `New-VM -Path` puts VM config + the multi-GB `.VMRS` on the same drive as the VHD, instead of defaulting to `C:\ProgramData` (which failed `Start-VM` on a full system drive).
2. **Grow-only resize** — `Resize-VHD` only grows a copied template, never shrinks it (shrinking a template with a larger virtual size corrupts the disk); fails fast with cleanup if the copy is unreadable.
3. **No checkpoint disk leak** — disable automatic checkpoints on lease VMs (client Hyper-V enables them by default), and `removeVM` always deletes the base VHDX, sweeps any `<name>_<guid>.avhdx`, and removes the per-VM config directory. Without this, every release leaked a disk-sized file.
4. **Honor explicit `--ssh-user`** — `applyDefaults` inherits an explicit SSH user before defaulting the guest user.
5. **TargetOS / explicit-target handling** — force `TargetOS=windows`; reject an explicit `--target` that conflicts with the provider's native OS; preserve explicit target config; never delete claims on a non-Windows host.
6. **Credential & robustness hygiene** — guest password via env var (not argv); fail fast on injection failure instead of burning the timeout; post-boot injection retry; populate `PublicNet.IPv4.IP`; shared Windows readiness check; doctor reports the image.
7. **Canonical name only** — removed unapproved provider aliases, keeping `hyperv`.

## Configuration

CLI flags: `--hyperv-image`, `--hyperv-cpu`, `--hyperv-memory`, `--hyperv-disk`, `--hyperv-switch`, `--hyperv-user`, `--hyperv-work-root`

YAML (`.crabbox.yaml`):
```yaml
hyperv:
  image: C:\Images\win-server.vhdx   # VHDX template with OpenSSH + a known user
  switch: Default Switch
  user: crabbox
  cpus: 4
  memory: 8192   # MB
  disk: 50       # GB; only grows the template, never shrinks
```

Env vars: `CRABBOX_HYPERV_IMAGE`, `CRABBOX_HYPERV_USER`, `CRABBOX_HYPERV_WORK_ROOT`, `CRABBOX_HYPERV_GUEST_PASSWORD` (for PowerShell-Direct injection).

## Not included (follow-up)

- **Configurable storage root** — storage currently follows `%USERPROFILE%`; a dedicated config knob (rather than profile redirection) would help hosts whose profile is on a full drive.
- **Differencing-disk leases** — each lease copies the full template; a differencing/parent-disk mode would avoid the per-lease copy.
- **ISO install / autounattend** — templates must be pre-provisioned VHDX; ISO is rejected.
- WSL2 mode is explicitly rejected (native Windows only).
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
